article.headline: Haaland fires Norway to verge of WC qualification
article.description: Erling Haaland scored again and Norway moved even closer to qualifying for the World Cup for the first time since 1998 by beating Estonia 4-1 in Oslo.
article.linkText: Haaland fires Norway to verge of WC qualification

article.story:
Erling Haaland scored again and Norway moved even closer to qualifying for the World Cup for the first time since 1998 by beating Estonia 4-1 in Oslo on Thursday.

The win virtually secures a spot for the high-scoring Norwegians in next year's tournament in the United States, Canada and Mexico. Norway has a vastly superior goal difference compared to second-placed Italy, ahead of the two sides meeting in their final qualifier in Milan on Sunday.

- Who has qualified, and how the rest can make it

Italy would have to beat Norway by nine goals to avoid having to settle for the playoffs, where the four-time World Cup champion was shockingly eliminated in the past two editions. 

All the goals came in the second half. Haaland scored twice in a six-minute span to increase the lead to 4-0 after Alexander Sørloth put the hosts 2-0 ahead shortly after the break.

Haaland has scored 14 of Norway's European-best 33 goals in seven group-stage games. The Manchester City striker was born two years after Norway last reached the World Cup.
